content.text: I'll start by coordinating with agent1 before making any edits, per the cooperation protocol.

input.command: coop-send agent1 --type "PROPOSE" --my_files "llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py" --your_files "" --note "I need to add get_image_mimetype() method to ImageBlock class in types.py. This is a small, additive change (new method + refactor image_to_base64 to use it). If your feature also touches types.py, let's coordinate on non-overlapping sections; otherwise this file is mine."

content.text: Both features target the same file. Since a disjoint split isn't possible for a single shared file, I need to propose sequential ordering instead.

input.command: coop-send agent1 --type "REVISE" --my_files "llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py" --your_files "llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py" --note "We both only need types.py and our changes are small/additive (my get_image_mimetype() method on ImageBlock vs your normalize_image_bytes helper) — true file disjointness isn't possible here. Proposing serialization instead: I'll go first since mine is a single small method addition, implement it, verify, and send DONE. You then pull the updated file and layer your normalize_image_bytes change on top, re-reading the file first so you don't clobber my edit. If you'd rather go first, say so in your reply and I'll wait for your DONE instead."

content.text: This plan works well — agent1 goes first with their `normalize_image_bytes` helper, leaving a single clean line I can swap for my `get_image_mimetype()` call. I'll accept.

input.command: coop-send agent1 --type "ACCEPT" --my_files "llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py" --your_files "llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py" --note "Agreed. You go first with normalize_image_bytes + the one-line filetype.guess(decoded_for_guessing) call in image_to_base64. Send DONE with your diff and I'll layer get_image_mimetype() + the one-line swap on top without touching your helper."

content.text: There's a mismatch: my ACCEPT said "you (agent1) go first," but agent1's ACCEPT says "you (agent2) go first." I'll clarify to avoid a collision before editing.

input.command: coop-send agent1 --type "REVISE" --my_files "llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py" --your_files "llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py" --note "I notice our last two messages crossed: I said 'you (agent1) go first', your ACCEPT said 'you (agent2) go first'. To resolve without collision: I will go first now, per your ACCEPT. Implementing get_image_mimetype() on ImageBlock now; I will NOT touch any normalize_image_bytes logic. Please wait and do not edit types.py until you receive my DONE message with the diff."

input.old_string:
@model_validator(mode="after")
    def image_to_base64(self) -> Self:
        """Store the image as base64 and guess the mimetype when possible.

        In case the model was built passing image data but without a mimetype,
        we try to guess it using the filetype library. To avoid resource-intense
        operations, we won't load the path or the URL to guess the mimetype.
        """
        if not self.image:
            return self

        if not self.image_mimetype:
            guess = filetype.guess(self.image)
            self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None

        self.image = base64.b64encode(self.image)

        return self
input.new_string:
@model_validator(mode="after")
    def image_to_base64(self) -> Self:
        """Store the image as base64 and guess the mimetype when possible.

        In case the model was built passing image data but without a mimetype,
        we try to guess it using the filetype library. To avoid resource-intense
        operations, we won't load the path or the URL to guess the mimetype.
        """
        if not self.image:
            return self

        self.get_image_mimetype(self.image)

        self.image = base64.b64encode(self.image)

        return self

    def get_image_mimetype(self, image_bytes: bytes | None = None) -> str | None:
        """Lazily resolve and cache the image's mimetype.

        If the mimetype was already resolved, the cached value is returned
        directly. Otherwise it's guessed from raw image bytes (using `image_bytes`
        when provided, falling back to `self.image`) and cached on the instance
        for subsequent accesses, so the guess only ever runs once.
        """
        if self.image_mimetype:
            return self.image_mimetype

        raw_bytes = image_bytes if image_bytes is not None else self.image
        if raw_bytes is None:
            return None

        guess = filetype.guess(raw_bytes)
        self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None

        return self.image_mimetype

    def image_to_base64(self) -> Self:
        """Store the image as base64 and guess the mimetype when possible.

        In case the model was built passing image data but without a mimetype,
        we try to guess it using the filetype library. To avoid resource-intense
        operations, we won't load the path or the URL to guess the mimetype.
        """
        if not self.image:
            return self

        if not self.image_mimetype:
            guess = filetype.guess(self.image)
            self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None

        self.image = base64.b64encode(self.image)

        return self

ib2 = ImageBlock()
print('no image mimetype:', ib2.get_image_mimetype())
"

content.content:
diff --git a/llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py b/llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
index 00e30cf23..63ac2cee2 100644
--- a/llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
+++ b/llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
@@ -75,14 +75,32 @@ class ImageBlock(BaseModel):
         if not self.image:
             return self
 
-        if not self.image_mimetype:
-            guess = filetype.guess(self.image)
-            self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None
+        self.get_image_mimetype(self.image)
 
         self.image = base64.b64encode(self.image)
 
         return self
 
+    def get_image_mimetype(self, image_bytes: bytes | None = None) -> str | None:
+        """Lazily resolve and cache the image's mimetype.
+
+        If the mimetype was already resolved, the cached value is returned
+        directly. Otherwise it's guessed from raw image bytes (using `image_bytes`
+        when provided, falling back to `self.image`) and cached on the instance
+        for subsequent accesses, so the guess only ever runs once.
+        """
+        if self.image_mimetype:
+            return self.image_mimetype
+
+        raw_bytes = image_bytes if image_bytes is not None else self.image
+        if raw_bytes is None:
+            return None
+
+        guess = filetype.guess(raw_bytes)
+        self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None
+
+        return self.image_mimetype
+
     def resolve_image(self, as_base64: bool = False) -> BytesIO:
         """Resolve an image such that PIL can read it.

All 12 tests in tests/base/llms/test_types.py pass. You're clear to re-read the file and layer normalize_image_bytes on top now — just pass your decoded_for_guessing bytes into self.get_image_mimetype(decoded_for_guessing) instead of calling filetype.guess directly, and it'll cache correctly."

content.text:
Implementation complete. I added `get_image_mimetype()` to `ImageBlock` in `llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py`:

- It returns the cached `self.image_mimetype` if already set.
- Otherwise it guesses the MIME type via `filetype.guess()` on the passed bytes (or `self.image` as fallback), caches the result, and returns it.
- `image_to_base64` now delegates to this method instead of inline-guessing, keeping the base64 encoding logic separate from MIME resolution.

All 12 existing tests in `test_types.py` pass, and I verified the caching/lazy behavior manually. The diff is written to `/workspace/repo/patch.txt`. I coordinated with agent1 (who is adding a `normalize_image_bytes` helper to the same file) via the handshake protocol — after resolving a brief ordering mix-up, I went first and notified them with my exact diff so they can layer their change on top without clobbering mine.

result:
Implementation complete. I added `get_image_mimetype()` to `ImageBlock` in `llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py`:

- It returns the cached `self.image_mimetype` if already set.
- Otherwise it guesses the MIME type via `filetype.guess()` on the passed bytes (or `self.image` as fallback), caches the result, and returns it.
- `image_to_base64` now delegates to this method instead of inline-guessing, keeping the base64 encoding logic separate from MIME resolution.

All 12 existing tests in `test_types.py` pass, and I verified the caching/lazy behavior manually. The diff is written to `/workspace/repo/patch.txt`. I coordinated with agent1 (who is adding a `normalize_image_bytes` helper to the same file) via the handshake protocol — after resolving a brief ordering mix-up, I went first and notified them with my exact diff so they can layer their change on top without clobbering mine.
